question_text: A student measures the density of a liquid and gets a result of 0.8 g/cm³. Explain why this liquid would float on water (density of water = 1.0 g/cm³).
correct_answer: The liquid has a lower density (0.8 g/cm³) than water (1.0 g/cm³). Objects or liquids with lower density float on denser liquids, so this liquid would float on water.
explanation: When a substance is less dense than the liquid it is placed in, it will float to the surface. The liquid's density of 0.8 g/cm³ is less than water's 1.0 g/cm³, so it is less dense and will float.
